title: RegLegBrief OKF Bundle — Confirmed AI Hallucinations on Primary Regulatory Text
description:
The complete corpus of confirmed AI hallucination findings from the RegLegBrief Specialist Panel, expressed in Google’s Open Knowledge Format (OKF) v0.1 specification. Every finding documents a delta between a named AI subject (Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Sonnet 4.6, etc.) and the regulator-issued primary-source text those subjects were tested against.

At a glance: 21 regulations across 8 regulatory bodies (IMF, OECD, BIS-CPMI, CFTC, FCA, MAS, UNTC), 107 confirmed hallucination findings each bound to a verbatim regulator excerpt, 4 jurisdictions (US, SG, UK, INT), 205 concept files.

Live bundle: reglegbrief.com/okf/ (regenerated from DB on every request — always current).
Hugging Face mirror: huggingface.co/datasets/verdus-tech/regleg-okf
Wikidata: Q140247788

Methodology and right-of-reply are open-access at reglegbrief.com/methodology/.
notes: Each new GitHub release on Verdus-Tech/regleg-okf is auto-deposited to Zenodo via the GitHub-Zenodo integration. The concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20716886 always resolves to the latest version. Title in this metadata file overrides Zenodo’s default auto-generated “Verdus-Tech/regleg-okf: <release title>” format on every future release.
method: Methodology: every finding passes through a Specialist-Panel-verified pipeline. (1) Substrate extraction from official regulator portals. (2) Asymmetric question generation spanning knowledge-mode, application-mode, embedded-wrong-premise, user-verification, source-contamination, and sycophantic patterns. (3) Sandboxed audit: subject AIs answer without any access to the substrate dir. (4) Verify-and-confirm: every hallucination is bound to verbatim regulator text before publication; the (c)-pattern methodology gate blocks any finding without a clean primary-source excerpt. Full disclosure at https://reglegbrief.com/methodology/
